title: Test: Cross-mesh communication via Yggdrasil
description:
## Goal

Validate that two separate LICHEN meshes can communicate via Yggdrasil backbone.

## Test setup

```
[Node A]              [Node B]
   │                     │
 LoRa                  LoRa
   │                     │
[Gateway A] ──Yggdrasil── [Gateway B]
   │                     │
(simulated or real Yggdrasil network)
```

## Test cases

### 1. Ping across meshes
- Node A: ping6 <Node B Yggdrasil address>
- Verify: reply received
- Validates: basic inter-mesh routing

### 2. CoAP request across meshes
- Node A: CoAP GET to Node B
- Verify: response received
- Validates: application-layer works

### 3. Dead Drop across meshes
- Node A: send dead drop to Node B
- Move Node B into range of Gateway B
- Verify: Node B receives message
- Validates: DTN + Yggdrasil integration

### 4. Gateway failure
- Send traffic A → B
- Kill Gateway A
- Node A associates with Gateway C
- Verify: traffic still flows
- Validates: gateway failover + Yggdrasil re-routing

### 5. Latency measurement
- Measure RTT: Node A ↔ Node B
- Compare: direct LoRa vs via Yggdrasil
- Document expected latency overhead

## Test environments

### Simulation
- Two separate simulator instances
- Connected via real Yggdrasil (or mock)
- Fast iteration

### Hardware
- Two RPi gateways on same LAN
- Real Yggdrasil peering
- T-Echo nodes on each mesh
- End-to-end validation
